pi_items.source: Verified via gnuhealth-50-demo.sql and setup_task.sh SQL query looking for patient 'Luna'
Verified via gnuhealth-50-demo.sql where party_party ID 11 has a NULL (\N) lastname
Verified via export_result.sh line 18 checking gpath.code LIKE 'O09%' OR 'Z33%' OR 'Z34%'
Verified via export_result.sh line 39 checking gpath.code LIKE 'Z57%'
Verified via export_result.sh line 67 checking pt.name for these case-insensitive strings
Verified via export_result.sh line 87 checking LIFESTYLE_INFO for these strings
Verified via verifier.py line 144 checking if lab_count >= 2
Found in task.json description as the agent's assigned role. Not present in the database (patient's occupation is NULL)
Not explicitly found in any script or database; loosely inferred from 'handles teratogenic chemicals in the lab'

privileged_info_summary: The target patient is Luna, with a NULL/empty last name (verified in the synthetic health50 SQL dump, party_party ID 11). The setup script dynamically injects a decoy 'Z33' pregnancy diagnosis into another patient, Ana Betz, as contamination. The verification scripts successfully enforce documentation of pregnancy (ICD-10 O09, Z33, or Z34) and occupational hazard (ICD-10 Z57) for Luna. It also requires prescribing a prenatal supplement (matching 'folic', 'vitamin', 'iron', or 'ferrous') and ordering at least 2 baseline labs. The lifestyle notes must contain restriction keywords ('restrict', 'reassign', 'teratogen', or 'hazard'). The 'industry' metadata claim 'Chemical Processing / Laboratory' is an unverified hallucination from the prompt.
